title: Measles Surging in Ontario
summary: Public health officials say Ontario is experiencing its worst outbreak of measles in decades.

title: Study links blood-brain barrier dysfunction and inflammation with Down syndrome regression disorder
summary: A new study led by Jonathan D. Santoro, MD, Director of the Neuroimmunology Program at Children's Hospital Los Angeles, shows evidence of dysfunction of the blood–brain barrier and inflammation in the central nervous system in individuals with Down syndrome regression disorder (DSRD).

title: The relationship between gut microbiota, immunoglobulin A, and vaccine efficacy
summary: Gut microbiota may be the key factor explaining why certain individuals do not respond well to the pneumococcal vaccine, a bacterium that can cause various diseases, such as pneumonia. This conclusion is drawn from a recent study led by the B Cell Biology Research Group at the Hospital del Mar Research Institute, published in Science Advances.

title: NICE recommends Gideon Richter’s Ryeqo as first daily pill for endometriosis
summary: Around 1,000 patients could benefit from the relugolix combination therapy every year

title: Improving rehabilitation after spinal cord injury using a small compound oral drug
summary: Spinal cord injury (SCI)—a condition that leads to partial or complete paralysis—has a profound impact on millions of individuals globally. Despite recent advances in SCI treatment, restoring lost motor functions, such as hand movement, remains a significant challenge.

title: Novel radiotracer identifies TNBC biomarker
summary: A new PET radiotracer can effectively visualize Nectin-4, an emerging biomarker found in triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC). According to new research, the PET tracer has the potential to play a critical role in the diagnosis, treatment, and monitoring of this aggressive disease, improving clinical outcomes for patients.

title: Eating seafood helps children develop better social behaviors
summary: Children who ate more seafood had better social skills. Omega-3, iodine, and selenium support brain function.

title: Treating Brain Injuries with Technology
summary: A recent article focused on the strides made in tackling a wide range of conditions that can arise as a result of a brain injury.

title: Researchers develop new compound to treat alopecia areata
summary: A research group, led by Dr. Pavel Majer from IOCB Prague, in collaboration with the laboratories of Barbara Slusher and Louis Garza at Johns Hopkins University, have developed a compound that could potentially treat the autoimmune disorder alopecia areata, which causes hair loss leading to the formation of bald patches.

title: New dental treatment designed to combat tooth decay and acid erosion
summary: Researchers at Queen Mary University of London have developed a new dental treatment to protect the teeth from decay and acid erosion, using cutting-edge civil engineering technology used to create the Forth Bridge paint.
